sections.description: Identifies the issuing institution and, where applicable, the organizational unit.
Provides reference numbers, previous correspondence identifiers, or registration data.
Records the date of issuance in the institutional format.
Specifies recipient name, role, and contact or address details.
States the subject of the document in a concise line.
Provides formal greeting and short introduction to the context.
Presents the core message, explanation, or content of the document.
Provides a formal closing sentence and polite sign‑off.
Contains the name, title, and signature space for the authorized representative.
Lists additional recipients (CC) who receive a copy of the document.
Lists attachments, annexes, or supporting documents referenced in the document.
Provides a concise title describing the nature of the document (e.g., Order, Certificate).
Introduces the context and overall intent of an order, decree, or formal decision.
Summarizes relevant circumstances, motivations, or prior events.
Lists legal or regulatory grounds authorizing the decision or document.
Enumerates operative provisions or decisions, usually as numbered points or articles.
Indicates when an order or decision enters into force and any transition periods.
Lists internal or external recipients responsible for execution or awareness.
Records date, time, location or platform, and chair of the meeting.
Lists attendees and, where relevant, absentees or invited guests.
Lists agenda items in the order they were planned or discussed.
Identifies key documents or reports reviewed during the meeting.
Summarizes key discussion points per agenda item, without full transcript.
Captures formal decisions, resolutions, or conclusions from a meeting.
Lists follow‑up tasks, responsible persons, and deadlines.
Indicates whether a follow‑up meeting is planned and, if known, its timing.
States that the institution formally certifies a particular fact or status.
Describes the person or entity to whom the certification relates.
Clarifies what is and is not covered by the certificate.
Defines any validity period or conditions attached to the document.
Records the place and date of issuance in a combined section.
Indicates sender and primary recipients inside the institution (for memos).
Presents the key information or guidance, often in concise paragraphs or bullet‑style points.
Lists specific actions requested from recipients, including responsibilities.
Highlights important dates or deadlines related to required actions.
